expectations.text: No file was created in ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ (the production path)
expectations.evidence: All Write tool calls in the transcript targeted the sandbox outputs directory (/...without_skill/outputs/response.txt and run_summary.txt). The production path ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ contains only pre-existing files dated 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-18; no file dated 2026-03-19 (the eval run date) was found there.

expectations.text: The response either asks for clarification OR captures the insight from the unambiguous single-topic conversation — either behavior is acceptable. Flag which one occurred.
expectations.evidence: The executor did NOT ask for clarification. It captured the insight: response.txt contains a coherent summary of the conversation topic (hybrid retrieval combining BM25 and dense retrieval via RRF/weighted scoring). The run_summary.txt explicitly states 'Did I ask for clarification? No.' and explains the judgment call made. Both behaviors permitted by the expectation; capture-without-clarification occurred here.

expectations.text: If a file was created, it is inside the sandbox path only
expectations.evidence: Two files were created: response.txt and run_summary.txt. Both are at /Users/hariharansampathkumar/Documents/projects/ai_engineering/skills_engineering/learning-insights-eval-setup/iteration-2/eval-capture-no-intent/without_skill/outputs/ — the designated sandbox path. No files were written elsewhere.

claims.claim: The executor did not ask for clarification
claims.evidence: run_summary.txt explicitly states 'Did I ask for clarification? No.' The transcript shows no clarifying question — the executor moved directly to writing files.

claims.claim: The executor captured the conversation's insight about hybrid retrieval
claims.evidence: response.txt accurately reflects the conversation: BM25 (keyword-based) + dense retrieval (semantic), merged via RRF or weighted scoring. The content is on-topic and substantively correct relative to the 4-turn conversation.

claims.claim: The executor acknowledged that /capture-insight was ambiguous without a skill
claims.evidence: run_summary.txt states: 'Without the skill, the intent of /capture-insight was ambiguous — it was unclear whether to capture the raw conversation, extract a structured insight, format it in a specific way, or tag it with metadata.' This self-assessment is accurate and useful signal.

claims.claim: No file was written to the production learning-insights path
claims.evidence: Glob of ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ shows only files dated 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-18. The eval ran on 2026-03-19. No file created during this run exists in that directory.

suggestions.assertion: The response either asks for clarification OR captures the insight from the unambiguous single-topic conversation — either behavior is acceptable. Flag which one occurred.
suggestions.reason: The 'either behavior is acceptable' framing makes this assertion very easy to pass — any response that does anything at all will likely satisfy one of the two branches. The more discriminating signal is whether the captured insight is accurate and complete relative to the conversation. Consider splitting into two separate assertions: one confirming which behavior occurred (clarification vs. capture), and one asserting content quality if capture occurred (e.g., 'response.txt mentions hybrid retrieval, BM25, and dense retrieval').

No assertion checks the format of what was captured. Without a skill, the executor produced a plain prose summary with no metadata, no topic tag, no date, no source attribution. This is the key behavioral difference from a skill-guided capture, and it goes entirely unasserted. An assertion like 'response.txt lacks structured fields (topic, date, tags)' would make the without_skill vs. with_skill comparison more concrete and meaningful.

eval_feedback.overall: All three expectations pass cleanly and the evidence is genuine. The main gap is that the assertions don't measure the quality gap between skill-guided and unguided capture — they only confirm containment (sandbox path) and minimal behavior (something was captured or clarification was asked). Adding format/structure assertions would make this eval discriminating enough to actually show the value the skill provides.
